item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is by far my favourite SCP of all time. Others are great, but this is my favourite. I'm a huge fan of simplicity and subtlety, and this does wonders in both aspects. Such an amazing read, and such a creative execution.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
small birthmark on its right shoulder

Do I smell a JoJo reference?

And to be fair, I think this would be a perfect villain for JoJo

item.title: Re: Art
item.content:encoded: This is absolutely fantastic. Thanks! ❤

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love how this could have been the standard psychopathic entity that wants to kill people, but it's just a completely ordinary guy who is understandably furious at his condition.

item.title: Art
item.content:encoded: Drew some art of this guy because he's my fave SCP!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Foundation researchers work in a place where they expect and actively look for anomalous activity, and which has a fair number of indestructible objects/creatures already. So his indestructibility would be perceived as an anomaly, as being anomalous is "within expectations for the situation". One possible interpretation is that he learned about the Foundation somehow and specifically sought them out, believing that they would understand him as anomalous and be able to get past the perception filter somehow (since he is understandably unhappy about his condition). He then became extremely disappointed when only the first part of that was true.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've been looking for this one! This is one of my favourites.

Probably perfect as-is, and test logs can't be added anyway since 1504 has never been re-contained, but they are very entertaining to imagine.

Test Log 1
Date: 6/28/19
Purpose: Determining an effective containment procedure.
Test: SCP-1504 subdued via administration of chloroform and locked in containment chamber.
Result: SCP-1504 successfully contained. Update 6/29/19: During routine maintenance the containment chamber was discovered to not contain SCP-1504. Subsequent analysis of surveillance video footage via optical imaging software developed in connection with SCP-592 revealed that SCP-1504 appeared to remain conscious after chloroform administration and walk out of the containment chamber before the door was closed and locked. SCP-1504 may be immune to chloroform. MTF ε-6 has been deployed to recapture SCP-1504

And eventually, after multiple iterations of this trying out different anaesthetics, different types of restraints, blunt trauma to the head, etc, some note from a researcher that they've started to expect SCP-1504 to breach containment, so personnel who open his containment cube report it as being empty, and there's a massive manhunt stretching across North America to find him, but they have no way of knowing whether he's really breached containment or is just wasting away in his cell imperceptible to all site personnel, and also to all new personnel introduced because their first introduction is via the SCP documentation stating he's uncontained….. etc.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If he's always perceived as normal, how did he come to The Foundation's notice in the first place?

He can't be harmed; but no researcher or investigator should have been able to perceive that as odd or noteworthy.

Say you've got a scenario like the start of Unbreakable with this guy as Bruce Willis. But because of his perception filter; reporters wouldn't bother to interview him, cops wouldn't write down the "completely normal" survivor's name, and the paramedics may not even bother to check if he's hurt in the first place. He wanders away from the train crash and the movie ends because Mr. Glass (The Foundation) doesn't have clue one that something odd happened.

item.title: Short movie
item.content:encoded:
They made a short movie about scp-1504. It's very good by the way.
youtube link

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: I am not satisfied with this crosslink, as I feel it doesn't add much to the piece, but I'd be interested in other suggestions.

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Edits vetoed by the author. Timer removed. No edits made at this time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Alright, so this object starts off as a simple AO entry. I feel that having the first mention of 1504 being an AO linking to the Log Of Anomalous Items not only would clarify what is exactly meant by that to a new reader, and lead them down that path of the wiki, but serves as a decent Wikiwalk platform given the links found in the AO list, as well as the AO tales that have gained popularity recently. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

Anomalous Object #: AO-1504-WGGYXJK

To this:

Anomalous Object #: AO-1504-WGGYXJK

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
MTF-Eta-6 (aka. Awareness Filters)

It was originally "Perception Filters." Named for the technology from Doctor Who that cloaks the tardis and a few other things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: where is the reference? i didn't catch it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ok, cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sort of, depending. See: tales.

Plus, tretter asked for permission on IRC and got approval to remove it anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … I don't think that's how tagging is supposed to work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Removed the tag 'indestructable' from this SCP so this can properly match my artistic intention and add a further layer of ambiguity to this piece.

item.title: Re: +320
item.content:encoded: To be fair, I was still gone when it happened.
There isn't a +300 post yet so…….. grats tretter!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is scary, yet, depressing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So here's what I think about SCP-1504:

+ Spoiler?
- yeah

I've always interpreted Joe as a sort of antimemetic warhead (kinda like Liz Day and Clay), but if whoever/whatever had created him really fucked up so that he's only a partially antimemetic entity/thing (sort of like SCP-055. It isn't completely invisible if you aren't under mnestics, but you instantaneously forget about it once you leave its chamber. The same thing applies here, as evidenced in the AO-1504 log). Maybe Joe has some sort of antimemetic radioactive field surrounding him that makes people interpret all his actions as being insignificant.

item.title: Funny thing, but...
item.content:encoded:
When I was going through the mainlist as I always do, one scip after another, I accidentally missed this one. I later got back to this one after reading the Dr. Wondertainment Chorebot scip & glancing back up a little XD

For those who didn't get why I thought that that was funny...
...let me explain

It's like the effect of this scip XD… I'll stop explaining the obvious now.

item.title: Re: +200
item.content:encoded: Woo!
congrats tretter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I read this soon after it was first posted, but didn't really getting it - over a year, a lot of reading up other stuff, and an understanding later, I can say this is friggin awesome.

EDIT:

Extensive research has shown that SCP-1504 can be incapacitated by blunt force trauma to the head.

I know it's not intentional - at least I think so - but this made me laugh.
Extensive research? Really? Just how extensive, exactly? Did you just hit him with something heavy?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually this bothers me now. Shouldn't his height be shorter so he would be even more unnoticeable? If he is a taller guy, he would get noticed more. So as Gene pointed out, he can't be overlooked in a crowd.

Make him shorter. Other than that, great article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Since I'm taking a semi-permanent leave from the wiki, I have asked FlameShirt to do something for me. If this SCP ever has to be rewritten, for whatever odd reason, I have asked him to rewrite it. I've seen what he can do, and I feel like he could work wonders with it. I'm not transferring authorship, it's simply who I want to rewrite it.

Regards,
Tretter

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It smacks a bit too much of SCP-451 for me, but that reveal at the end is pretty effective.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy crap. I mean, this is just great.

item.title: What the logs would actually look like...
item.content:encoded:
It occurs to me that after a while the guys would do nothing but stare loathingly at the person interviewing him. What's the point of talking if they're going to do and say whatever they want? After a few years of realizing you were essentially invisible you'd have a different take on life.

But thank goodness this guy is talkative so that we can get the exposition we need.

I never thought my first successful SCP would get so well received. At the time of me writing this, Joe Schmo has been translated into French and Russian. Thank you to you all for your votes and comments. I would have made a post at +100, but I never got around to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What if the site's content, community, and leadership are rapidly breaking down and going to hell in a handbasket, but we only think things are normal because of this article?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is fucking awesome. I had a very similar idea back when I joined the Foundation (guy whose every action is perceived as *obvious*, and something he should be doing by those around) but could never make anything out of it, and here you are giving something like that a great execution.
Hat off, and purr.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not a necropost, because you have an honest question and it isn't fluff. I used the format screw to emphasize how much the Foundation fucked up the job on this one. I also didn't want to use regular format because that would reveal too much at the beginning that I wanted to reveal later. So, good question.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not sure how far back is considered a necro-post, but I will post this anyway.
I don't understand why the format screw is necessary. I feel like the "recovered document" could almost just be a foldable below the description. Maybe I am just missing something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know this is an older comment, but I want to take the time to say why I didn't do logs. In universe, it's because the Foundation does not have 1504. If you look at the tags, one of them is "uncontained." From the standpoint of the author, I didn't write them because it would kind of destroy the mystery about SCP-1504. Also, Quatral, Dmatix and I might be writing a tale with Joe and the Old Man from Nowhere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I second this. Maybe a SCP story based off of it would be interesting?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great read, only wish there were more logs. Reading more about the actions of 1504 and what others perceive them to be would be a blast!

+1 with gusto

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd like to see Old Man(the one who's apparently god of the hunt) and this thing tangle. I just want to know if he effects the Old Man the same way. Then we can toss him to the protolizard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, I just hope indestructible guys don't become normal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I opened the page. Saw the indestructable guy alert. Thought "Wait, how the hell does a guy what can't be killed get this many upvotes?!?"
I read the page. Went "Hmmmmmm…"
Noticed that I had missed the two collapsables. Read both of them.
Holy shit.
+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When the page first loaded my eyes caught the bold letters. my first thought was, "Holy crap, thats unorthodoxed!" But I thoroughly enjoyed it. Biggest sell for me was definitely the level threes. Plus you took a cliché and made it unique. upvote 1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…Caucasian male, standing at 1.95 meters tall. The subject’s appearance is unremarkable…

Yeah, totally unremarkable. Can easily be overlooked in a crowd.

Other than that, a good and well-executed idea. I like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: MUCH better than the drafts I saw. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm impressed by how economical this article is. Most of the length is due to formatting; there's actually not that much in the way of text, and the bare-bones writing just points out how simple and sinister this idea is. Very well done!

item.title: Dialogue
item.content:encoded: The first recording seems pretty stiff for a conversation, even for a clinical type person. The second interview log of the same conversation is fantastic, but I feel like it's just a bit off. Other than that, +1 and if I could vote more I would. Wonderful article.

item.title: Re: hey
item.content:encoded: Somebody call Noah, I have a feeling this dudebro won't get the memo. Djoric, you just made my day with that comment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thoroughly enjoyed this article. The only thing that I could possibly advise against is the fact that the diction is a little strange. That, however, doesn't take away from the article itself one bit, in my opinion.]

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this article. +a million and then some more.
But one thing kinda jumps out as awkward- You're supposed to contact a project supervisor on sight, and then it proceeds to give one even mildly distinguishing feature about him.

Like, I get how it's a thing. I really do.
I don't quite know how to make it less a thing and keep the thing from doing that thing irksome (to me, at least) and still keep with the idea, but I'm just tossing that out there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No vote, because I think the format screw isn't really necessary (you can achieve the same effect by using correct, but vague description, and then gradually revealing more details in addenda), and the concept, while neat, doesn't do enough for me to just ignore the format.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The description feels a bit awkward, but overall it's really good. +1

item.title: Re: hey
item.content:encoded:
Look.

Dude.

Dudebro

Broseph.

If you are smart enough to type the site name into the address bar, you're smart enough to use proper grammar. Please do so.

Second, we have a forum. This is a discussion thread for SCP-1504, meant for talking about SCP-1504. It is not a place to ask random questions.

You can, but that does not mean that you should. This is a creative writing website. We take writing seriously here. You should first draft something up in that sandbox, get feedback, and revise. Posting an article right now is asking for one thing: a downvote flood of Biblical proportions.

item.title: Re: hey
item.content:encoded: You should put it in the sandbox and run it by forums and chat first.

item.title: hey
item.content:encoded: am i allowed to just post a scp

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Brrrr. I didn't know what the heck was going on until it was slammed in my face in the addendum. Which is pretty much what this SCP does. You get a +1 for actually making me shiver. That interview log really sold it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not really feeling it, and though the execution's decent, it leaves much to be desired. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it in chat and I liked it here. I still think we should just shove him in a centrifuge and be done with him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I guess I had a very basic idea of where the idea "ended" and the execution "began" when I posted that. *shrug*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty good, overall. I would suggest to have the new containment procedure put 1504 on an Area, rather than a site (as appropriate to a Keter object this dangerous), but it's not a deal breaker.
